SVNSEA2E.HeroPointsInfo: Your luck and drama currency. Spend one to activate a Virtue, gain a bonus, or bend a scene your way; earn them back when your Hubris or a Quirk complicates the story. Use −/+ to spend or gain one. (Core p.177)
SVNSEA2E.Name: Name
SVNSEA2E.ReputationInfo: A single word for how Théah sees your Hero — "Honorable", "Deadly", "Well-Connected". It adds dice to a Risk whenever it fits the moment socially. (Core p.163)
SVNSEA2E.RollsExploded: The dice exploded and 10s were rerolled.
SVNSEA2E.TraitDescBrawn: Raw physical power, toughness and stamina — lifting, grappling, enduring punishment and hitting hard. (Core p.133)
SVNSEA2E.TraitDescFinesse: Coordination, precision and speed — swordplay, marksmanship, sleight of hand and delicate work. (Core p.133)
SVNSEA2E.TraitDescResolve: Mental and physical fortitude — willpower, composure under pressure, and shrugging off fear and pain. (Core p.133)
SVNSEA2E.TraitDescWits: Sharpness of mind — perception, quick thinking, memory, cunning and reading a situation. (Core p.133)
SVNSEA2E.TraitDescPanache: Charm, flair and speed of action — social grace, leadership, wit and initiative. (Core p.133)
SVNSEA2E.TraitDescInfluence: A Villain's social reach and authority — wealth, station and the power to command others.
SVNSEA2E.TraitDescStrength: A Villain's or Brute's raw might and menace in a fight.
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescAim: Ranged attacks and marksmanship — bows, crossbows and firearms. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescAthletics: Running, climbing, jumping, swimming and general physical feats. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescBrawl: Unarmed and improvised fighting — fists, boots, bottles and grappling. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescConvince: Persuasion through reason, charm or negotiation — winning hearts and changing minds. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescEmpathy: Reading people's feelings and intentions, and understanding what drives them. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescHide: Concealing yourself or objects — stealth, camouflage and slipping out of sight. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescIntimidate: Bending others to your will through fear, force of personality or threat. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescNotice: Spotting details, danger and things others miss — awareness and searching. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescPerform: Music, acting, oratory and dance — entertaining and moving an audience. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescRide: Handling horses and other mounts, and driving carriages. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescSailing: Crewing and handling ships — rigging, knots, navigation and life at sea. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescScholarship: Book learning — history, science, law, languages and research. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescTempt: Seduction and enticement — playing on desire and vice to sway others. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescTheft: Stealing, picking locks and pockets, and other larceny. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescWarfare: Military knowledge — tactics, strategy, command and the tools of war. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SkillDescWeaponry: Melee combat with weapons — swords, knives, axes and the like. (Core p.142)
SVNSEA2E.SorceryDescHex: Eisen alchemy that brews grisly unguents to command and destroy the undead. (Core p.207)
SVNSEA2E.SorceryDescGlamour: The sorcery of Avalon, channeling the mantles of legendary Knights and pacts with the Sidhe. (Core p.214)
SVNSEA2E.SorceryDescPorte: Montaigne blood sorcery that tears bleeding portals between marked people and places. (Core p.220)
SVNSEA2E.SorceryDescSanderis: Sarmatian pacts with the dievas (devils), trading Corruption for elemental Deals. (Core p.224)
SVNSEA2E.SorceryDescSorte: The Vodacce Fate Witches' art of reading and weaving the strands of destiny (women only). (Core p.229)
SVNSEA2E.SorceryDescDarm: Ussuran sorcery from Matushka ("Mother's Touch"), granting powers tied to the land and its beasts. (Core p.217)
SVNSEA2E.SorceryGuideTitle: When may a Hero learn a sorcery?
SVNSEA2E.SorceryGuideGeneral: Sorcery isn't free-form. A Hero learns it only by buying their own nation's Sorcery Advantage — you can't pick up another people's magic, and each bloodline is tied to its nation. Every purchase teaches just a limited, fixed set of effects (not the whole tradition); it's repeatable to learn more. Some traditions also carry a price — Corruption, Villainy, or the law. (Core p.206-208)
SVNSEA2E.SorceryAcquireHex: Buy the Sorcery Advantage: each purchase teaches 1 Major + 2 Minor Unguent recipes. Practised almost only in Eisen and outlawed there in many places; the book warns it "can easily lead to Villainy," so tread carefully. (Core p.206-207)
SVNSEA2E.SorceryAcquireGlamour: The magic of the Knights of Avalon, taken via the Sorcery Advantage. Any Hero "called to the Graal" may become a Knight — no birthright or gender requirement — and each Glamour costs a Hero Point to activate. (Core p.213-214)
SVNSEA2E.SorceryAcquirePorte: Montaigne blood sorcery, taken via the Sorcery Advantage: each purchase connects you to 2 Minor + 1 Major Blood Mark. Opening a portal costs a Dramatic Wound, and opening a blessure always inflicts Corruption. (Core p.220-221)
SVNSEA2E.SorceryAcquireSanderis: Sarmatian pacts with the dievas (devils), taken via the Sorcery Advantage. Favors have a price: a Major Favor always costs 1 Corruption, and a broken bargain blocks every Favor until you make it good. (Core p.224)
SVNSEA2E.SorceryAcquireSorte: Vodacce Fate magic of the Strega — women only — taken via the Sorcery Advantage. Every Weave costs a Hero Point and at least one Lash as Fate takes its toll, and a Strega can never target herself. (Core p.229)
SVNSEA2E.SorceryAcquireDarm: Ussura's Mother's Touch, taken via the Sorcery Advantage: each purchase builds a Lesson of 2 Gifts + 1 Restriction. A Gift works only while you keep its Restriction — break it and you lose your Gifts until you perform the Penance. (Core p.217-218)
SVNSEA2E.WealthInfo: Buys life's extras — you can always afford basic food, shelter, and clothing for free. Starts at zero each session; earn it by plying a Profession (gain Wealth equal to the Skill you use). Mostly lost between sessions — you keep at most half. Click a taler to set it. (Core p.164)
SVNSEA2E.WealthAfford3: Enough to hire a Brute Squad for a scene.
SVNSEA2E.WealthAfford5: Enough for something scarce or illegal.
SVNSEA2E.WealthAfford8: Enough to buy a business, house, or ship.
SVNSEA2E.WealthRerollHint: In a money-driven social Risk, click a die to reroll it — each bribe spends 1 Wealth ({n} left).
SVNSEA2E.WealthRerollNote: Spent 1 Wealth to reroll a {old} into a {nu}. {n} Wealth left.
SVNSEA2E.WealthRerollInfo: Only legal in a social Risk that money could influence — bribes and gifts. Each Wealth Point spent rerolls one d10; don't use it on combat or other pools. (Core p.164)
SVNSEA2E.OneSocietyOnly: A Hero may belong to only one Secret Society (Core p.258). Remove the current one first.
SVNSEA2E.DuelStyleNeedsAcademy: A Dueling Style is learned through the Duelist Academy Advantage (Core p.236). Added anyway — make sure the Hero has bought it.
SVNSEA2E.WealthLevel1: Buys a high-quality common item — an excellent sword, a trustworthy horse. In a money-driven social Risk, each Wealth spent rerolls one d10. (Core p.164)
SVNSEA2E.WealthLevel2: A couple of fine goods, or two rerolls in a money-driven social Risk. (Core p.164)
SVNSEA2E.WealthLevel3: Hires a Strength 5 Brute Squad for a scene. (Core p.165)
SVNSEA2E.WealthLevel4: More than a Brute Squad, not yet enough for scarce or illegal goods. (Core p.165)
SVNSEA2E.WealthLevel5: Buys something non-unique that isn't readily available, or is outright illegal. (Core p.165)
SVNSEA2E.WealthLevel6: A growing hoard, building toward real property. (Core p.165)
SVNSEA2E.WealthLevel7: Almost enough for a business, a house, or a ship. (Core p.165)
SVNSEA2E.WealthLevel8: Buys a business, a house, a ship, or similar property. (Core p.165)
SVNSEA2E.WealthLevel9: Property, with a little coin to spare. (Core p.165)
SVNSEA2E.WealthLevel10: The top of the guideline scale. Remember: you lose at least half your Wealth at the end of each session. (Core p.165)
SVNSEA2E.AtLeastOptions: You must choose at least {min} options.
SVNSEA2E.NoMoreThanOptions: You may choose no more than {max} options.
SVNSEA2E.ItemExists: The {type}: {name} is already assigned to the character.
SVNSEA2E.ItemDoesntExist: {name} doesn't exist in the world's items.
SVNSEA2E.WrongNation: The character's nation ({anation}) doesn't match the required background {name}'s nation ({bgnation}).
SVNSEA2E.GMOnly: This tool is for the Game Master.
SVNSEA2E.RiskBuilderIntro: Frame a Risk for the table: set the scene, then list what it costs (Consequences) and what it offers (Opportunities). Post it to chat so players know what their Raises can buy. (Core p.172-173)
SVNSEA2E.RiskSituationHint: Set the stage: describe the scene and the elements that could help or hinder the Hero, before anyone rolls. (Core p.172)
SVNSEA2E.RiskSituationPlaceholder: e.g. The room is on fire, three storeys up. Tapestries, a chandelier on a rope, a suit of armour…
SVNSEA2E.RiskApproachHint: The Hero states what she wants and how; the GM names the Trait + Skill to roll. Acting outside that Approach later costs an extra Raise. (Core p.172-173)
SVNSEA2E.RiskConsequencesHint: What the Risk does to the Hero — every Risk has at least one. The Hero spends Raises to overcome or reduce them; each unspent one still lands. (Core p.172-173)
SVNSEA2E.RiskOpportunitiesHint: Extra benefits the Hero can seize by spending Raises. Not every Risk has one. (Core p.172-173)
SVNSEA2E.RiskTimeLimitHint: Optional. This triggers once everyone drops below this many Raises — Raises must be spent on it before then, or it happens. Leave blank for none. (Core p.179)
SVNSEA2E.RiskRaisesHint: How many Raises the Hero must spend to overcome this Consequence or seize this Opportunity.
SVNSEA2E.RiskOppPlaceholder: e.g. Grab the secret letter before it burns
SVNSEA2E.RiskExamplesHint: Worked examples from the rulebook (Core p.174) for common Actions — pick one to add a row, then edit it. Most Consequences and Opportunities should emerge from the Scene.
SVNSEA2E.RiskSceneIntro: Add each Hero taking part and the Trait + Skill (their Approach) they'll roll. In an Action Sequence everyone rolls their OWN pool against this one shared Risk — the Hero with the most Raises acts first, then the next, until everyone's Raises are spent. (Core p.179)
SVNSEA2E.RiskSceneHint: Who is in the scene and the pool each will roll. One Risk, many rolls — this is not a separate Risk per Hero.
SVNSEA2E.RiskNoHeroes: No Heroes added yet — click "Add Hero" to list who's in the scene.
SVNSEA2E.RiskNoPcActors: No player-character Heroes exist yet. Create some, then add them to the scene.
SVNSEA2E.RiskPoolHint: This Hero's dice pool for the Risk — Trait value + Skill value in d10.
SVNSEA2E.RiskNameHint: A short label so you can find this Risk in your library later (e.g. "Burning warehouse", "Ballroom duel").
SVNSEA2E.RiskNamePlaceholder: Name this Risk to save it…
SVNSEA2E.RiskNewHint: Clear the form and start a fresh Risk.
SVNSEA2E.RiskNeedsName: Give the Risk a name before saving it.
SVNSEA2E.RiskDeleteConfirm: Delete the saved Risk "{name}"? This cannot be undone.
SVNSEA2E.RiskNoConsequences: Add at least one Consequence — every Risk has one.
SVNSEA2E.RiskNoOpportunities: No Opportunities (that's fine — not every Risk has one).
SVNSEA2E.RiskNeedsContent: Describe the Situation or add a Consequence first.
SVNSEA2E.RiskExample: John's Hero sprints across the burning room — a Brawn + Athletics Risk. Consequence: take 2 Wounds. Opportunity: grab the secret letter for 1 Raise. He rolls his pool and makes 3 Raises — 1 to cross the room, 1 to grab the letter, 1 to negate a Wound. He still takes the other Wound. (Core p.172-174)
SVNSEA2E.BonusDiceInfo: Extra dice you've earned this Risk (Flair, Core p.176): +1 for using a Skill for the first time this Scene, +1 for a vivid description or clever quip, plus any dice from Reputation or Advantages. Set the total here — it's added to the pool.
SVNSEA2E.DwBonusInfo: A Hero with one or more Dramatic Wounds gains +1 Bonus Die on every Risk (Core p.165). Added to the pool automatically.
SVNSEA2E.ExplodeInfo: Each die that rolls a 10 adds another d10, which can explode again (Core p.165). Automatic at 3+ Dramatic Wounds; some sorcery and Advantages grant it too.
SVNSEA2E.JoieInfo: Joie de Vivre (Core p.154): just before a confrontation with a Villain, spend a Hero Point and make a witty, defiant remark. On the next roll, every die that lands at or under your Skill counts as a 10 — for you and any ally who heard you. Costs 1 Hero Point, and you must own the Advantage.
SVNSEA2E.JoieRequires: Requires the Joie de Vivre Advantage.
SVNSEA2E.HeroPointDiceInfo: Spend Hero Points on this roll — each one adds a bonus die to your pool (Core p.177). You can't spend more than you have; the points are deducted from your sheet when you roll. (Gain and adjust Hero Points on the character panel.)
SVNSEA2E.NotEnoughHeroPoints: Not enough Hero Points — that would spend {need}, but you have {have}.
SVNSEA2E.RollerGuideTitle: How rolling works — when, what & how
SVNSEA2E.GuideStep1: The GM calls a Risk and names a Trait + Skill — that pairing is your Approach. (p.172)
SVNSEA2E.GuideStep2: Build your pool: Trait + Skill, plus any Bonus Dice (a fresh Skill or a vivid description each add one — that's Flair — as do Reputation and some Advantages), then roll. (p.176)
SVNSEA2E.GuideStep3: Make Raises: group the dice into sets that each total 10 — every set is one Raise. Dice you can't fit into a 10 are wasted. (p.172)
SVNSEA2E.GuideStep4: Spend your Raises to take your Action, beat Consequences and seize Opportunities. No Raises? The GM narrates it going wrong. (p.173)
SVNSEA2E.PoolEmpty: Choose a Trait and Skill to build a dice pool.
SVNSEA2E.SorceryNotLearned: Take the Sorcery Advantage to learn it.
SVNSEA2E.NoSorceryEffects: No sorcery effects found for this tradition.
SVNSEA2E.ToggleTheme: Switch between the Paper and Night at Sea themes
SVNSEA2E.SettingThemeHint: Choose the palette for Théah character sheets. Paper is the default aged sea-chart look; Night at Sea is the dark variant.
SVNSEA2E.PacksSeeded: Seeded {count} entries into the Théah compendia.
SVNSEA2E.CompendiumOnly: {type} items must come from a compendium — pick or drag one from the Théah compendia instead of creating a custom.
SVNSEA2E.BrowseCompendium: Browse compendium (drag an entry onto the sheet)
SVNSEA2E.WizConceptIntro: Who is your Hero? Choose a name, a Nation and a concept. Your Nation shapes your Trait bonus, starting language and which sorcery is open to you.
SVNSEA2E.WizTraitsIntro: Every Trait starts at Rank 2. Spend your 2 free points, then take your Nation Bonus (+1 to one of two Traits).
SVNSEA2E.WizBackgroundsIntro: Pick two different Backgrounds. Each grants ranks in its five Skills, its Advantages, and a Quirk. Overlapping Skills rise to Rank 2.
SVNSEA2E.WizSkillsIntro: Spend 10 Skill points on top of your Backgrounds. No Skill may go above Rank 3 at creation.
SVNSEA2E.WizAdvantagesIntro: You have 5 points to buy Advantages. Advantages from your Backgrounds are already granted for free.
SVNSEA2E.WizArcanaIntro: Choose one Virtue and one Hubris. They need not come from the same arcanum.
SVNSEA2E.WizProwessIntro: Your fighting school and your allegiances. Learn a Dueling Style if you took the Duelist Academy Advantage, and — if your story allows — join a single Secret Society.
SVNSEA2E.WizDuelStyleHint: Your Duelist Academy training taught you one Style — choose it.
SVNSEA2E.WizNoDuelistAcademy: Buy the Duelist Academy Advantage (in the Advantages step) to learn a Dueling Style.
SVNSEA2E.WizSocietyHint: Optional. A Hero may belong to only one Secret Society — click to join, click again to leave. It costs nothing but loyalty.
SVNSEA2E.WizReplacementHint: Both your Backgrounds granted the same Advantage. Take another Advantage of the same point cost instead — your choice. (Core p.137)
SVNSEA2E.WizNeedReplacement: Choose a replacement Advantage for each duplicate Background Advantage.
SVNSEA2E.WizLanguagesInfo: Your Hero speaks a number of languages equal to Wits. Old Théan and your native tongue are free; pick the rest here. (Core p.163)
SVNSEA2E.WizLanguagesHint: Pick additional languages up to your Wits (click to add or remove).
SVNSEA2E.WizLanguageCap: You may know only {n} languages (equal to your Wits).
SVNSEA2E.WizNeedDuelStyle: Choose a Dueling Style — you purchased the Duelist Academy Advantage.
SVNSEA2E.WizNationInfoHint: The +1 applies to one of these Traits after your 2 free points. National sorcery is bought later as an Advantage. (Core p.137)
SVNSEA2E.WizNoCompendia: The core compendia haven't loaded yet. Ask your GM to open the world once so the Backgrounds, Advantages and Arcana are seeded.
SVNSEA2E.WizPlayerOnly: The Hero Creator is only for player characters.
SVNSEA2E.WizNeedBonus: Choose which Trait gets your Nation Bonus.
SVNSEA2E.WizNeedArcana: Choose a Virtue and a Hubris.
SVNSEA2E.WizAdvBudget: That would exceed your 5 Advantage points.
SVNSEA2E.WizOpenFailed: The creator couldn't open — press F12 and check the Console for the error.
SVNSEA2E.RaisesInfo: Roll a pool of d10 equal to your Trait + Skill, then group the dice into sets that each total 10 or more — every set is one Raise (a single 10 counts, and a set may exceed 10). Dice you can't fit into a 10 are wasted. Spend Raises to act, beat Consequences and seize Opportunities. (Core p.172)
SVNSEA2E.TraitInfo_brawn: Raw physical power and toughness — hitting hard, grappling, lifting and shrugging off harm.
SVNSEA2E.TraitInfo_finesse: Speed, agility and precision — swordplay, marksmanship and delicate handiwork.
SVNSEA2E.TraitInfo_resolve: Willpower, grit and composure — enduring pain, fear and temptation.
SVNSEA2E.TraitInfo_wits: Intelligence and perception — reasoning, noticing details and reacting quickly.
SVNSEA2E.TraitInfo_panache: Charm, daring and flair — leadership, first impressions and dramatic style.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_aim: Ranged and thrown attacks — bows, pistols and a steady hand.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_athletics: Running, climbing, jumping, swimming and raw physical feats.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_brawl: Unarmed fighting — fists, grapples and improvised weapons.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_convince: Winning people over with reason, honesty or negotiation.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_empathy: Reading emotions and motives — and sensing when someone lies.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_hide: Stealth, concealment and blending into a crowd.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_intimidate: Bending others through fear, threats or sheer presence.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_notice: Spotting hidden details, danger and things out of place.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_perform: Music, acting, oratory and captivating an audience.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_ride: Handling horses and other mounts, even under stress.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_sailing: Crewing and steering ships — rigging, knots and navigation.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_tempt: Seduction and stirring desire or greed in others.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_theft: Sleight of hand, lockpicking and picking pockets.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_warfare: Tactics, command and knowledge of the battlefield.
SVNSEA2E.SkillInfo_weaponry: Melee combat with swords, knives and hand weapons.
SVNSEA2E.DwEffect1: Death Spiral: +1 Bonus Die on your Risks.
SVNSEA2E.DwEffect2: Death Spiral: Villains roll 2 extra dice against you.
SVNSEA2E.DwEffect4: Death Spiral: Helpless — you can no longer act.
SVNSEA2E.DwTip1: Gain a +1 Bonus Die on every Risk you make. (Core p.165)
SVNSEA2E.DwTip2: Villains gain 2 Bonus Dice on Risks made against you. (Core p.165)
SVNSEA2E.DwTip3: Your dice that land on 10 explode — roll again and add. (Core p.165)
SVNSEA2E.DwTip4: You are Helpless: you can no longer act until you heal. (Core p.165)
SVNSEA2E.CorruptionInfo: Comes from Evil Acts (1st = +1, 2nd = +2, 3rd = +3, 4th = +4, up to 10). Each time you gain it the GM rolls 1d10 — if it's ≤ your Corruption, your Hero falls from grace and becomes a Villain. A 5-step Redemption Story removes 1. (Core p.203)
SVNSEA2E.EvilActHint: GM only. The Hero commits an Evil Act: they gain escalating Corruption (1st +1, 2nd +2, 3rd +3, 4th +4), then the GM rolls 1d10 — a result of the Hero's Corruption or less means they Fall from Grace and become a Villain. (Core p.203)
SVNSEA2E.EvilActConfirm: {name} commits Evil Act #{act}, gaining {gain} Corruption (total {total} / 10). The GM will then roll 1d10 — a result of {total} or less turns the Hero into a Villain. Proceed?
SVNSEA2E.EvilActConfirmMaxed: {name} is already at maximum Corruption (10 / 10). Any further Evil Act is certain damnation — the GM rolls 1d10 and the Hero Falls from Grace. Proceed?
SVNSEA2E.FallFromGrace: {name} Falls from Grace — the Hero becomes a Villain!
SVNSEA2E.CorruptionRisk: Fall from Grace looms — a 1d10 of {n} or less would turn the Hero Villain.
SVNSEA2E.AdvStepsHint: Click a Step to mark it done as you achieve it in play.
SVNSEA2E.AdvRewardIntro: You don't spend experience — Heroes grow by finishing Stories. Choose the one Reward this Story earns; its cost sets how many Steps the Story takes.
SVNSEA2E.AdvConfigureIntro: Pick exactly what this Story earns.
SVNSEA2E.AdvStoryIntro: Name your Story and write its Goal — the specific deed that ends it — plus the first Step on the road there.
SVNSEA2E.AdvNewQuirkPlaceholder: e.g. Earn a Hero Point when your pride puts you in danger.
SVNSEA2E.AdvCorruptionIntro: Redemption is never easy or quick. This five-Step Story sheds a single point of Corruption.
SVNSEA2E.AdvStoryNamePlaceholder: A short name or mantra for this Story
SVNSEA2E.AdvGoalPlaceholder: The concrete deed that completes this Story.
SVNSEA2E.AdvFirstStepPlaceholder: The first action that puts you on the road to your Goal.
SVNSEA2E.AdvReviewFoot: Start this Story, then tick off its Steps on your sheet. Claim the Reward when the last Step is done.
SVNSEA2E.AdvH_skillRaise: Improve one Skill by one Rank (up to 5).
SVNSEA2E.AdvH_traitIncrease: Raise one Trait by one Rank. Only twice, ever.
SVNSEA2E.AdvH_traitShift: Move one Rank from one Trait to another.
SVNSEA2E.AdvH_advantage: Buy an Advantage from the compendium (also how you gain Sorcery).
SVNSEA2E.AdvH_arcanaChange: Swap your Virtue or Hubris for another.
SVNSEA2E.AdvH_quirkChange: Replace one of your Quirks with a new one.
SVNSEA2E.AdvH_corruptionRemove: Shed a single point of Corruption.
SVNSEA2E.AdvNoSkillRoom: All your Skills are already at Rank 5.
SVNSEA2E.AdvNoTraitRoom: No room to raise or shift Traits (max Rank 5, 15 total, two increases).
SVNSEA2E.AdvNoCompendia: The core compendia haven't loaded yet.
SVNSEA2E.AdvNoBackgrounds: You have no Backgrounds with a Quirk to change.
SVNSEA2E.AdvNoCorruption: You have no Corruption to remove.
SVNSEA2E.AdvNeedReward: Choose a Reward for this Story.
SVNSEA2E.AdvRewardUnavailable: That Reward isn't available right now.
SVNSEA2E.AdvSkillMaxed: That Skill is already at Rank 5.
SVNSEA2E.AdvTraitMaxed: That Trait is already at Rank 5.
SVNSEA2E.AdvTraitMinned: That Trait is already at its minimum (Rank 2).
SVNSEA2E.AdvTraitTotalCap: You can have at most 15 total Ranks of Traits.
SVNSEA2E.AdvTraitTwice: You may only increase a Trait twice, ever.
SVNSEA2E.AdvPickBothTraits: Pick a Trait to raise and a Trait to lower.
SVNSEA2E.AdvDistinctTraits: The two Traits must be different.
SVNSEA2E.AdvPickBackground: Pick which Background's Quirk to change.
SVNSEA2E.AdvAlreadyClaimed: This Story's Reward has already been claimed.
SVNSEA2E.AdvNotComplete: Finish every Step before claiming the Reward.
SVNSEA2E.AdvSourceMissing: Couldn't find that item in the compendium anymore.
